title: F.Essentials
description: F.Essentials is a feature-rich mod for Fabric that brings essential server commands and utilities to Minecraft. Designed to make server management and player interaction easier.
body:
# F.Essentials
> **This is not a fully functional version of the mod, but rather a pre-alpha release.**
>  The mod is not intended for regular use at this stage, but rather for testing purposes or to assist with development. Any feedback, help, or suggestions are greatly appreciated.

**F.Ess** is a reimplementation of the core features found in EssentialsX plugin, designed specifically for Minecraft **Fabric**. This mod provides essential server management tools and quality of life features, making it easier for server admins and players to manage worlds on Fabric. With features like teleportation, home setting, private messages, spawn setting, and more. **FEss** offers a lightweight and efficient solution for both admins and players. 

One of the primary goals of **FEss** is to preserve **most commands and privileges** from the **EssentialsX** plugin, ensuring a smooth and easy transition for server admins and players migrating from a plugin-based server environment to Fabric.

*Whether you're new to Fabric or transitioning from EssentialsX, **F.Ess** brings familiar commands to a mod-based ecosystem, providing continuity and ease of use.*

## Support & Development
If you would like to support the development, you can contribute any amount through my [Ko-fi page](https://ko-fi.com/fnewell), or alternatively, you can test the mod and share your feedback (or help/suggestions) on [Discord](https://discord.gg/akZp7ZD8VZ).

You can track the current development of the mod (implemented/planned commands, updates, changes, etc.) on the [Notion page](https://fnewell.notion.site/f-essentials).


## Notes:
- **FEssentials** is not associated with or a port of the **EssentialsX** plugin. It is a reimplementation of similar core commands and privileges, designed for Fabric to facilitate an easier transition from plugin-based environments to mod-based ones.

- Currently, the mod is licensed under ARR, and the code is not public. In the future, I would like to make the code public and turn the project into an open-source initiative, but at the moment, I don't feel confident enough. I see this project as a learning experience and an opportunity to improve my skills and get into Fabric modding. However, I would be very grateful for any feedback on Discord.
